lever_summary: Dense i64 ascending sort/argsort via a sign-flipped stable LSD radix that bypasses the generic SortKey/Literal machinery. The new sort_along_axis_dense_i64 gathers each slice's i64 values straight from as_i64_slice(), radix-orders them (O(n)), and writes a dense i64 output — no Vec<Literal> materialization, no per-element sort_key, no Literal write-back.
diagnosis: An initial in-place LSD radix that reused the generic indexed (orig_idx, SortKey) pipeline gave NO win (10.83ms vs 10.50ms baseline): the hot path is dominated by the per-element SortKey/Literal machinery, not the comparison count. Per the no-ceiling addendum this signalled optimizing the wrong thing; the fix was the data-model bypass (dense gather + raw-u64 radix + dense output), combined with the O(n) algorithm.

alien_graveyard.primitive: radix sort (non-comparison, O(n) for fixed-width integer keys) + dense SoA gather
alien_graveyard.mapping: replace the comparison sort (O(n log n)) over a 24-byte tagged-union with a stable LSD radix over contiguous native i64, the integer-key analog of the pattern-defeating/radix sort techniques

method: Same-worker-calibrated A/B: baseline = tensor_ops.rs git-stashed to the comparison sort; an unchanged calibration bench (full i64 reduce) ran alongside in both runs to normalize cross-worker CPU speed. speedup = (baseline_sort/baseline_calib) / (after_sort/after_calib).
command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'sort_64k_i64|sort_calib_reduce_64k_i64' --save-baseline {sortB|sortA2}

change.lever: Added sort_along_axis_dense_i64 (gather as_i64_slice -> (sign-flipped u64 key, in-slice index) pairs -> 8-pass stable LSD radix -> dense i64 output). sort_along_axis calls it first when dtype==I64 && !descending; returns None (generic path) for descending / non-I64 / non-dense / axis<256.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: for I64 the generic comparator is a.cmp(&b); the sign-flipped (v as u64 ^ 1<<63) byte order reproduces signed i64 order exactly; LSD radix is stable and the per-slice gather visits in-slice indices ascending, so equal keys keep their original order, matching the stable sort_by
isomorphism.direction: ascending only; descending falls back to the comparison sort (whose stable tie order a radix-then-reverse would not preserve)
isomorphism.outputs: sort emits the reordered i64 values; argsort emits the in-slice original indices — the same fields the generic path writes; output dtype I64 (sort) / I64 (argsort)
isomorphism.tie_breaking: stable, identical to the generic stable sort
isomorphism.errors: returns None (generic path) for non-dense / non-I64 / short axes / non-dividing shapes; no new error classes

validation.name: radix_sort_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib sort
validation.result: passed: 12 tests incl radix_sort_i64_ascending_matches_comparison_sort (n=1000 with negatives/dups/i64::MIN/MAX, sort + argsort vs Rust stable sort, + [4,300] multi-slice per-row)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
